headline: Trump says Apple to partner with Intel on US chip design, production
summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said in a Truth Social post on Thursday that Apple has agreed to ‌work with Intel to design and manufacture its chips in ‌the United States.  A partnership with Intel helps Apple diversify its manufacturing base as it seeks ​additional chip capacity.  The iPhone maker relies heavily on TSMC, whose advanced production lines are in high demand from AI chipmakers such as Nvidia and AMD.

content.title: Trump says Apple to partner with Intel on US chip design, production
content.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said in a Truth Social post on Thursday that Apple has agreed to ‌work with Intel to design and manufacture its chips in ‌the United States.  A partnership with Intel helps Apple diversify its manufacturing base as it seeks ​additional chip capacity.  The iPhone maker relies heavily on TSMC, whose advanced production lines are in high demand from AI chipmakers such as Nvidia and AMD.

headline: Wall Street on track to open with gains, oil falls and Intel soars on Trump post about Apple deal
summary: U.S. markets rose in premarket trading Thursday, taking back much of their losses from a day earlier that were driven by speculation that the Federal Reserve may raise interest rates this year to try and reign in persistent inflation.  The Nasdaq was on track to claw back all of its losses from Wednesday, with futures gaining 1.3% before the opening bell.  Oil prices continued their charge downward after the U.S. and Iran signed their initial agreement ending the war.

headline: Stocks rise on Wall Street, erasing much of their loss from a day earlier
summary: The gains are helping to cut losses from a day earlier that were driven by anticipation that the Federal Reserve will likely raise interest rates this year in an effort to fight inflation.  Intel surged 7% after President Donald Trump announced that the semiconductor giant will make chips for Apple in the U.S. Other big semiconductor companies gained ground.  On the losing end, SpaceX fell for the second straight day since its ballyhooed debut on the U.S. stock market last week.

headline: Intel shares rise after Trump announces Apple partnership
summary:
STORY: ::Intel

::Archive

Shares of Intel rose as much as nearly 12% on Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said the company would partner with Apple on chip design and production...

:: Intel

in what would be a major boost for Intel's efforts to turn around its business. 

A contract with Apple would lock in steady demand from the iPhone maker, boosting Intel's reputation and sales as it looks to close the gap with rival TSMC, the world's biggest contract chipmaker.

::Archive

::AMD

For Apple, the deal would help diversify its manufacturing base and add chip capacity at a time when TSMC, its key partner, has been stretched thin by surging demand from AI chip-making clients such as Nvidia and AMD.

Trump's social media post about the agreement did not specify which chips Intel would make for Apple.

The president said it was his latest effort to help Intel, in which the U.S. government has a 10% stake.

::Archive

The Trump administration has been stepping up efforts to secure U.S. supply chains for semiconductors and critical minerals, including taking equity stakes in companies to reduce reliance on China.

Apple and Intel did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

content.title: Intel shares rise after Trump announces Apple partnership
content.summary:
STORY: ::Intel

::Archive

Shares of Intel rose as much as nearly 12% on Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said the company would partner with Apple on chip design and production...

:: Intel

in what would be a major boost for Intel's efforts to turn around its business. 

A contract with Apple would lock in steady demand from the iPhone maker, boosting Intel's reputation and sales as it looks to close the gap with rival TSMC, the world's biggest contract chipmaker.

::Archive

::AMD

For Apple, the deal would help diversify its manufacturing base and add chip capacity at a time when TSMC, its key partner, has been stretched thin by surging demand from AI chip-making clients such as Nvidia and AMD.

Trump's social media post about the agreement did not specify which chips Intel would make for Apple.

The president said it was his latest effort to help Intel, in which the U.S. government has a 10% stake.

::Archive

The Trump administration has been stepping up efforts to secure U.S. supply chains for semiconductors and critical minerals, including taking equity stakes in companies to reduce reliance on China.

Apple and Intel did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

summary: Intel on Thursday named Seok-Hee Lee executive vice president of its contract chip-manufacturing division ‌as it sharpens focus on its advanced packaging business.  The ‌American chipmaker has been trying to reinvigorate its manufacturing business under CEO ​Lip-Bu Tan, after missing out on the AI boom.  The appointment follows President Donald Trump's announcement earlier in the day that Apple had agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture ‌its chips in the ⁠U.S. in a boost to the chipmaker's contract manufacturing business.
